sequences.title: Conference Contact Outreach Sequence
sequences.targetAudience: Credit union executives met at industry events
sequences.approvalNote: Approved by Martha and Shella. A/B versions per email keyed to contact title. Email 2 collaborative invite angle well-received. Timing rationale approved as-is.
sequences.usageNote: Evergreen. Use for any contact made at an in-person or virtual industry event, on an ongoing basis. Not tied to a batch or hypothesis test. Do not use for cold contacts or warm intro referrals.

sequences.title: Cold Outreach — Community Bank VP/EVP (Southeast)
sequences.targetAudience: VP/EVP at community banks, $500M–$1B assets, Southeast & Mid-Atlantic
sequences.usageNote: Angle: invisible attrition — deposit relationships lost when the primary account holder dies. Do not use for event contacts, CDFIs, or institutions over $2B. Same sender (Roland Mercer) as seq-003 and seq-004 — check recipient overlap before sending.

sequences.title: Cold Outreach — DMI Metrics Education Series
sequences.targetAudience: Retail, deposit, and experience leadership at banks and credit unions, national, bank-agnostic
sequences.usageNote: Restructured from the original 5-part version — now Parts 1-3 only (education arm), single version per email, direct peer-to-peer voice. Names Prismm, what it does, and the Deposit Mortality Index starting in Part 1. Each subsequent part anchors to one real DMI metric, corrected against the live getprismm.com methodology: Growth Coverage Ratio (Part 2, trailing 3-yr growth vs. annualized mortality runoff), ten-year dollar/percent runoff (Part 3). CTAs are direct offers, no false urgency. Followed by seq-004 (Parts 4-5, benchmark/direct-ask) roughly 2-3 weeks later, same campaign. Requires the DMI $799 institution report to be live and fulfillable before sending. Do not use for warm event contacts (seq-001) or the loss-framed VP/EVP Southeast track (seq-002).

sequences.title: Cold Outreach — DMI Peer Benchmark
sequences.targetAudience: Retail, deposit, and experience leadership at banks and credit unions, national, bank-agnostic
sequences.usageNote: Follow-on to seq-003, same campaign (CAMP-DMI-01), different intent — formerly Parts 4-5 of the original 5-part seq-003. Leads with the composite DMI score (succession capture deficit, peer percentile ranking) rather than teaching a metric. Send only to contacts who completed seq-003 (or received a restart-trigger re-introduction), roughly 2-3 weeks after seq-003's Part 3, so it reads as its own beat rather than continuation fatigue. CTAs are direct offers with real timeframes, no false urgency. Requires the DMI $799 institution report to remain live and fulfillable. Do not use for warm event contacts (seq-001) or the loss-framed VP/EVP Southeast track (seq-002).
